How's it going everyone? In this video we're going to cover how to upload a video, make sure it's unlisted, and then where the link is for that video so that way you can put in your tracking dock or use it wherever you need to. So in the address bar at the top left of your browser, like Google Chrome as a browser, you'll want to click it in there and you see the blinking cursor there. If there's any text in there, go ahead and hit the backspace or delete to get rid of it. We're going to go and type in youtube.com and hit enter on the keyboard. It'll take us right to the YouTube page. Now if you're not signed into YouTube, you won't see a little portrait picture up there. Go ahead and get signed in first, that way it has an account to go to. When you're signed in, you should see this little camera with a plus sign on it. It says create a video or post when you move your mouse over it. Go ahead and click on that. And in the menu that opens up, go ahead and click on upload video right there. It's going to take you to this screen. The first thing we want to do is change the viewing of this video. Right now it's set to public, so we're going to go ahead and click on that. We're going to change it to unlisted. Unlisted means that this video is not going to show up in your YouTube channel, but anybody with a link is able to see it. That way if somebody goes to your YouTube channel, they're going to see all these videos. It's very important to do that, especially if you're doing personalized videos. It helps keep your video channel looking clean. So once you have that drop down set to unlisted, you want to move your mouse over this big arrow pointing up. It turns red when you move your mouse over it. And I'm going to go ahead and click on it. Now this is a Windows computer, so it looks a bit different. But go ahead and pick the video you want. And once you pick that, double click it. And it should start uploading it right away. Now this is a pretty small video. It's going to be done really quick. But if you have a very large video, especially one with a lot of motion or very high quality, it's going to take a long time for that processing. You actually don't have to wait for the processing to be done to get the link to it. So right here where it says your video will be live at, I'm going to click and drag with my mouse to highlight that, the HTTPS YouTube. And once that's all highlighted, I'm going to hit Control C on my keyboard if you have a Mac, it's Command C to copy. And if you have a tracking document open, go to that tracking document, click where you want to put it and paste it in there. That way you have that link later on. I always recommend keeping eye a good list of all of your links. That way you can track them down pretty easily. Now this actually takes you right to it. So I'm going to show you when you open a new tab. If you paste in that link, it should take you right to that placeholder video. This is just a basic video that I did. You want to go ahead and use a real video, of course. This is going to be used as part of most plays, especially when you're going to have a video as part of the announcement. And you're also going to use the same process if you're creating custom videos to send other people. It's a pretty easy process, but hopefully you should be able to get it tackled. Thanks for watching and hope you have a great rest of your day.
